zh.lifeSummary: 當James De Clifford於1390年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 白 希雷斯特時，他的父親Henry Clifford30歲，母親Joan Hore29歲。他於1415年與Grace Foliot在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 白 希雷斯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1468年在他的家鄉享年78歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 白 希雷斯特。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When James De Clifford was born in 1390, in Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry Clifford, was 30 and his mother, Joan Hore, was 29. He married Grace Foliot in 1415, in Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1468, in his hometown, at the age of 78, and was buried in Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England.

Il a épousé Grace Foliot en 1415, à Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. ja.lifeSummary: James De Cliffordが1390年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン ビー シアーエンセスターで生まれた時，父Henry Cliffordは30歳，母Joan Horeは29歳であった。1415年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン ビー シアーエンセスターでGrace Foliotと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1468年に故郷で78歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン ビー シアーエンセスターにて埋葬された。

ko.Frampton, Gloucestershire, , England: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터
ko.lifeSummary: James De Clifford(이)가 1390년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Clifford(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Hore(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1415년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터에서 Grace Foliot(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1468년 그의 고향에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James De Clifford родился в 1390 г. в местности фрамптон би сиренсестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry Clifford было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Hore, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Grace Foliot в 1415 г. в местности фрамптон би сиренсестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1468 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности фрамптон би сиренсестир, глоусестиршир, Англия.
